name: Marine Engineering
description: Marine Engineering deals with the design, construction, and maintenance of ships, offshore structures, and marine propulsion systems. It integrates mechanical, electrical, and naval architecture principles for maritime applications.

subjects.name: Marine Propulsion
subjects.description: Study of marine propulsion systems including diesel engines, gas turbines, steam turbines, and nuclear propulsion. Covers propulsion theory, power transmission, shafting systems, and propeller design.
learning_outcomes: Compare different marine propulsion systems for efficiency and application
Design propeller systems for optimal thrust and cavitation control
Analyze shaft alignment and vibration in propulsion trains
Evaluate fuel consumption and emissions from propulsion plants
subjects.industry_relevance: Core technology for all commercial and naval vessels including container ships, tankers, and warships.

subjects.name: Ship Construction
subjects.description: Covers shipbuilding processes, structural design, welding techniques, and material selection for hull construction. Includes block construction methods, launching techniques, and shipyard operations management.
learning_outcomes: Design hull structures using classification society rules
Plan block construction and assembly sequences for shipbuilding
Apply welding procedures for hull plate and stiffener joining
Manage shipyard production scheduling and quality control
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for shipbuilding yards, offshore fabrication facilities, and marine repair industries worldwide.

subjects.name: Naval Architecture
subjects.description: Fundamentals of naval architecture including hydrostatics, stability, resistance, and seakeeping. Covers ship geometry, buoyancy calculations, intact and damage stability, and ship resistance prediction methods.
learning_outcomes: Calculate hydrostatic properties and stability criteria for ships
Analyze intact and damage stability using regulatory standards
Predict ship resistance using empirical and computational methods
Evaluate seakeeping performance in various sea conditions
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for ship design firms, classification societies, and regulatory bodies like IMO and flag states.

subjects.name: Marine Engines
subjects.description: In-depth study of marine internal combustion engines including two-stroke and four-stroke diesel engines. Covers engine construction, fuel injection, turbocharging, combustion analysis, and engine performance monitoring.
learning_outcomes: Analyze thermodynamic cycles of marine diesel engines
Troubleshoot fuel injection and combustion system issues
Optimize turbocharger matching for engine performance
Conduct engine performance trials and emissions measurement
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for shipping operations, as diesel engines power over 90% of the world's commercial fleet.

subjects.name: Marine Auxiliary Systems
subjects.description: Covers shipboard auxiliary machinery including pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, separators, steering gear, and deck machinery. Includes system design, maintenance, and automation of auxiliary systems.
learning_outcomes: Design seawater cooling and bilge systems for marine vessels
Select pumps and compressors for marine service conditions
Maintain hydraulic and pneumatic systems for deck machinery
Automate auxiliary system controls for unmanned engine room operation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for vessel operations, maintenance, and classification society compliance across all ship types.

subjects.name: Ship Stability
subjects.description: Advanced study of ship stability including dynamic stability, large angle stability, stability criteria, and regulatory requirements. Covers stability in damaged conditions, grain loading, and heavy lift operations.
learning_outcomes: Calculate righting levers and stability curves for intact and damaged ships
Apply IMO intact stability criteria for vessel certification
Assess stability during cargo loading and ballasting operations
Evaluate damage stability using probabilistic methods
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory knowledge for ship masters, naval architects, and classification surveyors ensuring vessel safety.

subjects.name: Marine Electrical Systems
subjects.description: Study of marine electrical power generation, distribution, and protection systems on ships. Covers generators, switchboards, transformers, motors, power electronics, and emergency power systems.
learning_outcomes: Design shipboard power generation and distribution systems
Configure switchboard protection and automation schemes
Troubleshoot marine electrical machinery and control systems
Apply IEC and classification rules for marine electrical installations
subjects.industry_relevance: Increasingly important with electrification of ships, diesel-electric propulsion, and battery hybrid systems.

subjects.name: Ship Design
subjects.description: Comprehensive ship design process from concept to detailed design including hull form design, general arrangement, and systems integration. Covers design spiral, regulatory compliance, and cost estimation.
learning_outcomes: Develop ship design specifications from owner requirements
Optimize hull form for resistance and seakeeping performance
Produce general arrangement plans for various ship types
Estimate shipbuilding costs and construction timelines
subjects.industry_relevance: Central to ship design consultancies, naval ship programs, and commercial shipbuilding projects worldwide.

subjects.name: Marine Pollution Control
subjects.description: Covers marine pollution prevention, regulations (MARPOL), oil spill response, ballast water treatment, and exhaust gas cleaning systems. Includes environmental monitoring and waste management onboard ships.
learning_outcomes: Apply MARPOL annex regulations for shipboard pollution prevention
Design ballast water treatment systems meeting D-2 standards
Develop oil spill contingency plans for port and offshore operations
Evaluate exhaust gas cleaning systems for sulfur emission compliance
subjects.industry_relevance: Increasingly stringent environmental regulations drive demand for pollution control expertise in shipping.

subjects.name: Marine Refrigeration
subjects.description: Study of refrigeration and air conditioning systems for marine applications including cargo refrigeration, provision cooling, and HVAC. Covers refrigerant selection, system design, and energy efficiency optimization.
learning_outcomes: Design marine refrigeration systems for reefer cargoes
Select environmentally friendly refrigerants per F-gas regulations
Calculate cooling loads for accommodation and cargo spaces
Optimize HVAC system operation for fuel efficiency
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for reefer shipping, LNG carriers, and passenger vessel comfort systems.

subjects.name: Ship Dynamics
subjects.description: Study of ship motions in waves including heave, pitch, roll, and maneuvering characteristics. Covers seakeeping theory, added resistance in waves, motion control systems, and maneuvering prediction.
learning_outcomes: Predict ship motion responses using strip theory and panel methods
Design roll stabilization systems including fins and tanks
Analyze ship maneuvering characteristics using MMG model
Evaluate added resistance in waves for powering requirements
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for seakeeping analysis of naval vessels, passenger ships, and offshore support vessels.

subjects.name: Maritime Law
subjects.description: Overview of international maritime law including UNCLOS, SOLAS, MARPOL, and STCW conventions. Covers ship registration, maritime liens, salvage law, insurance, and admiralty jurisdiction.
learning_outcomes: Interpret international maritime conventions applicable to ship operations
Analyze liability and compensation frameworks for maritime incidents
Apply charter party terms and bills of lading in commercial shipping
Evaluate marine insurance policies and claims procedures
subjects.industry_relevance: Legal framework knowledge required for ship management, marine insurance, and maritime dispute resolution.

subjects.name: Marine Safety
subjects.description: Study of maritime safety management including ISM Code, FSA, emergency preparedness, and life-saving appliances. Covers fire safety, stability in damaged condition, and safety culture in shipping operations.
learning_outcomes: Develop safety management systems compliant with ISM Code
Conduct formal safety assessments for maritime operations
Design fire-fighting systems and arrangements for ships
Prepare contingency plans for emergency scenarios including abandon ship
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory for all commercial vessels under SOLAS, driving continuous improvement in maritime safety culture.

subjects.name: Port & Harbor Engineering
subjects.description: Covers design and construction of ports, harbors, docks, and navigational infrastructure. Includes breakwater design, dredging, terminal planning, cargo handling equipment, and port management systems.
learning_outcomes: Design breakwaters and quay walls for port protection
Plan container terminal layouts for efficient cargo operations
Analyze dredging requirements for navigation channel maintenance
Evaluate port capacity and throughput optimization strategies
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing global trade drives demand for port infrastructure development and modernization projects.

subjects.name: Ocean Engineering
subjects.description: Study of offshore structures, ocean energy systems, subsea engineering, and marine renewable energy. Covers wave-structure interaction, mooring systems, riser design, and offshore installation methods.
learning_outcomes: Design fixed and floating offshore platforms for various water depths
Analyze mooring systems for station keeping of floating structures
Evaluate ocean energy conversion systems including wave and tidal
Design subsea pipelines and risers for deepwater applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for offshore oil and gas, offshore wind, and emerging ocean renewable energy sectors.

subjects.name: Underwater Acoustics
subjects.description: Study of sound propagation in water, sonar systems, underwater communication, and acoustic positioning. Covers transducer technology, signal processing, and marine bioacoustics for naval and civilian applications.
learning_outcomes: Model sound propagation in shallow and deep water environments
Design sonar systems for detection and classification applications
Process acoustic signals for underwater positioning and communication
Assess environmental impact of anthropogenic noise on marine life
subjects.industry_relevance: Used in naval defense, offshore survey, underwater navigation, and marine research applications globally.
